name: Sa'd ibn Hanzala
title: Warrior of Bani Tamim
shortDescription: A brave warrior from the Bani Tamim tribe who struck fear into the hearts of the enemy army with his epic war poetry in Karbala.
fullStory:
Sa'd ibn Hanzala al-Tamimi was a brave Shia and warrior from the Bani Tamim tribe. With a heart full of faith and devotion to Wilayah, he came to Karbala to help Imam Hussain (AS). Sa'd was famous for his bravery and combat skills among his people.

On the day of Ashura, after a group of the Imam's companions were martyred, Sa'd came to Imam Hussain (AS) and asked for permission to go to the battlefield. He entered the field with a tall posture and a drawn sword, standing before the Kufan army.

Sa'd recited an epic and moving war poetry in the battlefield: 'We practice patience and endurance in this battle... O Hussain, O our guide to the truth...'. With these verses, he declared his loyalty to the Imam and the path of truth.

With unparalleled skill, he attacked the heart of the enemy army, sparking a fierce and long battle. His swordsmanship was so powerful that the Kufans dared not approach him and were forced to attack him collectively.

Finally, after inflicting heavy casualties on the enemy and receiving numerous wounds, this brave Tamimi warrior fell to the ground defending Wilayah and joined the caravan of Karbala's martyrs.
quoteArabic: صَبْراً عَلى الأَسْيافِ وَالأَسِنَّةِ صَبْراً عَلَيْها لِدُخُولِ الجَنَّةِ
quote: I endure swords and spears, an endurance to enter Paradise.
